name: Place Pulse 2.0 - Urban Perception (6 Dimensions)
description: Pairwise comparison survey measuring six perceptual dimensions of street scenes: safety, liveliness, beauty, wealth, depressingness, and boringness. Replicates the crowdsourcing methodology of the Place Pulse 2.0 dataset (1.17M comparisons, 56 cities).
author: Dubey et al.

config.title: Place Pulse 2.0 – Urban Perception Survey
config.description: For each pair of street images, choose the one that looks more [safe / lively / beautiful / wealthy / depressing / boring]. Based on: Dubey, A., Naik, N., Parikh, D., Raskar, R., & Hidalgo, C. A. (2016). Deep learning the city: Quantifying urban perception at a global scale. ECCV 2016.
